name: Govindananda
dates_notes: Usually placed around the fifteenth to sixteenth century from the printed Advaita sub-commentarial tradition, but secure biographical dates are not available.
core_thesis: Govindānanda is the Advaita author associated with the *Bhāṣya-ratnaprabhā*, a widely transmitted gloss on Śaṅkara's *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya*. His entry represents the instructional and commentarial layer through which Śaṅkara's terse Brahma-sūtra exegesis is made usable for later students. The work does not mark a new lane like Bhāmatī or Vivaraṇa; it belongs to the broader Advaita sub-commentarial tradition, clarifying the bhāṣya's wording, argument sequence, and doctrinal implications. A GRETIL plaintext of the *Brahmasūtra* with the Ratnaprabhāvyākhyā was fetched into the local source corpus.

engaged_works.title: Bhasya-ratnaprabha
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Traditionally associated with Govindānanda in the Advaita printed and GRETIL/Sansknet cataloguing tradition; the local source header preserves the transmitted Ratnaprabhāvyākhyā title.
engaged_works.summary: A Sanskrit gloss on Śaṅkara's *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya*, transmitted as the *Bhāṣya-ratnaprabhā* or *Ratnaprabhāvyākhyā*. The work follows the Brahma-sūtra sequence and explains Śaṅkara's comments in a compact scholastic style, helping later readers track topic boundaries, objections, replies, and doctrinal conclusions in the bhāṣya. It is included here as a representative of the non-sectarian Advaita sub-commentarial layer rather than as the foundation of a separate sub-school. The GRETIL plaintext fetched for this pass is under `data/sources/sanskrit/vedanta/govindananda_bhasya_ratnaprabha_gretil.txt`.
